narrative:
The Karnataka High Court heard **816 of 1,942 scheduled matters**, an overall hearing rate of **42.0%**. That is a clear improvement on **24 April’s 31.5%**, even though absolute hearings were slightly lower (**879 to 816**), because the listed load fell sharply from **2,794 to 1,942**. In short, today’s list was materially lighter and converted more efficiently.

Judge-wise, the strongest high-volume performances came from **Chief Justice & C.M. Poonacha**, who together heard **44 of 51 matters (86.3%)**, **V Srishananda** with **59 of 82 (72.0%)**, **Anant Ramanath Hegde** with **43 of 59 (72.9%)**, and **K.S. Hemalekha** with **29 of 43 (67.4%)**. **M.I. Arun** also posted a solid **52 of 87 (59.8%)**. On the heavier end, **M. Nagaprasanna** had the largest listed burden at **368 matters** but heard **95 (25.8%)**, while **H.T. Narendra Prasad** heard **33 of 190 (17.4%)**. **K. Manmadha Rao** and **B M Shyam Prasad** recorded no hearings on their listed matters.

Stage conversion was strongest in **UNKNOWN (48.7%)**, **ORDERS (46.3%)**, and **ADMISSION (44.7%)**. These three stages accounted for about **64% of listings** but nearly **71% of all hearings**, showing that disposal was concentrated in earlier or procedural stages. By contrast, **FINAL HEARING (28.5%)** and **FURTHER HEARING (27.1%)** remained the weakest converting stages, indicating slower movement in more advanced matters. Compared with 24 April, **ORDERS** improved sharply from **24.2% to 46.3%**.

By case type, **WP** dominated the docket with **917 listings** and **326 hearings (35.6%)**, while **CRL.P** also remained below average at **35.8%**. Together, these two categories made up nearly **63% of all scheduled cases** but only **53% of hearings**, pulling down overall clearance. Better-converting sizable categories included **MFA (51.6%)**, **RSA (50.0%)**, **RFA (56.2%)**, and especially **CRL.RP (72.7%)**. Against 24 April, **WP** was broadly stable (**36.4% to 35.6%**), while **MFA** improved from **43.8% to 51.6%** and **WA** rebounded from **5.2% to 40.0%** on a much smaller list.
